title: What Happens When Curiosity Becomes a Startup?
excerpt: Muhammad Habeeb Abayomi is a student entrepreneur who believes the most meaningful work begins long before anyone is paying attention. Through technology, business, and community building, he is steadily pursuing a vision of creating organizations that solve problems, create opportunities, and leave a lasting impact.
content:
Building Before Graduation

Most people who meet Muhammed Habeeb Abayomi know him as a Computer Science student. What they often discover later is that he spends as much time thinking about businesses as he does software.

Growing up, he was fascinated by large organizations, the kind that outlive their founders and shape industries for decades. While many people dream of building a successful startup, Habeeb became interested in something larger: creating systems, companies, and opportunities that could continue growing long after he is gone.

That curiosity has led him down many paths.

He has worked on software products, organized entrepreneurial initiatives, collaborated with developers, participated in innovation programs, and spent countless hours learning how businesses are built. Along the way, he discovered that building a company is rarely as glamorous as it appears. Most of it involves uncertainty, difficult decisions, missed expectations, and the willingness to keep moving forward anyway.

One of the projects that emerged from this journey is Glass Finance. Rather than seeing it as a destination, Habeeb views it as part of a broader mission: using technology to solve real problems while creating opportunities for others.

What drives him is not simply the idea of launching products. It is the possibility of creating organizations that provide value, create jobs, and contribute to economic growth. Whether through technology, entrepreneurship, or future ventures in entirely different industries, he is motivated by the challenge of turning ambitious ideas into practical realities.

For now, he remains a student balancing classes, projects, leadership responsibilities, and startup ambitions. But he believes that some of the most meaningful journeys begin long before anyone is paying attention.

He is still learning. Still building. Still figuring things out.

And perhaps that is the most honest part of the story.

The goal has never been to become successful overnight. The goal is to keep building, keep learning, and eventually create something that makes a lasting difference.
